text.title: Google Search Ads Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-search-ads methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each ad group MUST contain 10-20 keywords sharing a single buying intent.
rule.rationale: Mixed intent dilutes RSA relevance; QS drops; CPC rises.

rule.statement: Each ad group MUST have ≥3 RSAs with diversified headlines (no shared first headline).
rule.rationale: 3 RSAs let Google's blender pick; identical headlines kill the test.

rule.statement: Match-type mix MUST include broad (with smart bidding only) + phrase + exact for the core keyword list.
rule.rationale: Pure broad over-explores; pure exact misses long-tail demand.

rule.statement: Keywords with QS <7 MUST be flagged for ad copy refresh or ad-group split within 7 days.
rule.rationale: QS multiplies effective CPC; chasing the auction without fixing QS burns budget.

rule.statement: Each campaign MUST ship ≥4 sitelinks + ≥4 callouts + 1 structured snippet category.
rule.rationale: Extensions lift CTR 15-30% at zero marginal cost.
